what_it_does: Defines a Pydantic contract for a node type, specifying the canonical shape that UI, runtime, and tools use to describe a node, including its kind, ports, schemas, render modes, and actions.
observations: Uses Pydantic BaseModel for validation and serialization.
Defines RenderMode and Kind as Literal string enumerations.
Ports holds two dicts mapping port names to string port-type names.
NodeType combines identity, classification, inheritance, ports, config/output/inspector schemas, render modes, actions, layout hints, and version.
render_set defaults to all four render modes.
actions defaults to a single-item list ['run'].
model_post_init fills an empty title from the node's name using str.title().
standouts: Aims to be the single source of truth for what a node is across UI, runtime, and tools.
Includes an extends field for a type-graph relation.
Separates input and output ports explicitly.
Supports multiple named render modes in one model.
conventions: Docstring references a contract identifier C2 and build-prep/contracts/C2.
Uses Pydantic Field with default_factory for mutable defaults.
model_post_init follows Pydantic v2 initialization hook style.
concerns: config_schema, output_schema, inspector_schema, and layout_hints are untyped dicts, so their contents are not validated by this contract.
extends is a plain string with no in-file resolution or validation.
Port types are strings with no registry or reference enforcement.
No constraints linking kind to render modes or actions.
version is present but no migration or compatibility logic is shown.
notes: This file is the canonical Pydantic node-type contract, meant to be projected by UI, runtime, and tools alike.
questions: What is the S4 type-graph relation referenced by extends?
How are port-type names resolved against a registry?
What is the precise meaning and behavior of each RenderMode?
What schema language or shape is expected inside config_schema, output_schema, and inspector_schema?
Which consumers enforce or extend this contract?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to provide the single definition of what a node is, and the file does that by declaring a single Pydantic model that captures node identity, classification, ports, schemas, render modes, and actions.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to establish a portable, tool-agnostic node descriptor that editors, executors, and utilities can all read and project, while leaving room for specialization via inheritance and multiple UI render modes.
contribution: Provides a standardized, serializable schema for node metadata and structure, giving UI, runtime, and tooling a shared vocabulary for describing nodes without embedding implementation details.
summary_for_embedding: A Pydantic contract in company/contracts/node_type.py that defines NodeType, the canonical descriptor for a node with kind, ports, schemas, render modes, actions, and version, intended for use across UI, runtime, and tools.
intention_for_embedding: To establish a shared, extensible node-type schema that UI, runtime, and tools can all project from.

symbols.does: Defines the input and output port dictionaries for a node.
symbols.description: Ports models a node's attachment points as two mappings, inputs and outputs, each associating a port name with a string port-type name.

symbols.does: Defines the canonical Pydantic schema for a node type.
symbols.description: NodeType captures everything needed to describe a node kind: identity, title, category, kind, inheritance, ports, editable and output schemas, supported render modes, inspector schema, actions, layout hints, and version.

symbols.does: Auto-assigns a title from the node's name when no title is provided.
symbols.description: After model initialization, if the title field is empty, this hook sets it to the node's name converted to title case, supplying a default display label.
